REIS IA BRAND GUIDELINES DESIGN SYSTEM v1.0

Brand Guidelines

The foundational rules that govern how Reis IA presents itself across every touchpoint. Every visual decision serves premium positioning, authority, and conversion.

[01] Design Philosophy

Five principles govern every visual decision. They are ordered by priority — when two principles conflict, the higher-ranked one wins.

01

Restraint Is the Design

Every element must earn its space. If removing something does not reduce comprehension or conversion, remove it. Black space is not emptiness — it is the primary visual device that signals premium positioning.

02

Typography Is Architecture

The type system does 90% of the hierarchy work. Headlines are large, confident, and tightly tracked. Body text is generous and readable. The contrast between dense impact and open legibility creates the visual rhythm of every page.

03

Dark Mode Mastery

This is not "white on black." It is a layered surface system where depth is communicated through subtle background value shifts, semi-transparent borders, and restrained glow effects. Shadows are nearly invisible. Elevation comes from background lightness, not shadow darkness.

04

Conversion Through Clarity

CTAs must be immediately visible. Visual flow must guide the eye toward the next action. The blue accent exists primarily for conversion elements. Every page leads to /agendar or /aplicar. The design never lets a visitor wonder "what should I do next?"

05

Engineered Precision

Every value is intentional. No default ease. No arbitrary spacing. No round-number border-radii when optical tuning produces a better result. The design system is engineered like the AI systems we build for clients.

[02] Brand Voice & Tone

Reis IA communicates with the authority of a senior strategist and the clarity of a trusted advisor. The voice is direct, confident, and outcome-focused — never salesy or generic.

Authoritative

Speaks from experience and proven methodology. Never tentative or speculative.

Direct

Gets to the point. Respects the reader's time. No filler words or corporate jargon.

Outcome-Focused

Every statement connects to a measurable result. Features are always framed through the lens of business impact.

Premium

Signals exclusivity through restraint. Fewer words, more weight. The writing mirrors the design — spacious and intentional.

[03] Brand Personality

Reis IA is a high-ticket AI consultancy, not a SaaS product. The brand personality reflects a senior advisor who commands premium fees because of proven results and systematic methodology.

Playful
Serious
Casual
Professional
Approachable
Exclusive
Technical
Strategic
Verbose
Concise
[04] Context & Positioning

Reis IA is an ecosystem focused on implementing and monetizing Artificial Intelligence for businesses, led by Moroni Reis. The ecosystem spans four pillars:

Builder

Direct AI implementation for businesses

Systems

Scalable AI systems and automation

Partners

Strategic AI partnerships

Network

Community and knowledge sharing

Key Rule

Every visual element serves premium positioning, authority, and conversion toward booking calls. All CTAs lead to /agendar or /aplicar. There are no pricing tables, tier cards, or SaaS-style layouts.

[05] Brand Guidelines — Dos & Don'ts

Color Usage

Do
  • Use blue accent for CTAs, brand icons, and emphasis moments. Max 2 blue elements per viewport.
  • Use text-secondary (70% white) for body copy. Full white (100%) is for headlines only.
  • Alternate sections between Surface-0 (#000) and Surface-1 (#0A0A0A).
Don't
  • Use blue for body text, borders (except featured card top border), or background fills. Blue is earned, not scattered.
  • Use hex grays (#A3A3A3, #737373) for text. Use the opacity scale instead for consistency across surface tiers.
  • Use more than one surface tier jump between adjacent sections. Surface-0 to Surface-3 is too jarring.

Typography

Do
  • Use text-wrap: balance on headings. Use text-wrap: pretty on body paragraphs.
  • Cap body text width at 680px. Cap headline width at 900px.
  • Tighten letter-spacing as font size increases (Display: -0.03em, Body: 0).
Don't
  • Use ALL CAPS except for Label tokens (badges, section labels). Headlines are sentence case.
  • Allow body text to stretch across the full 1200px container. Wide text blocks destroy readability.
  • Use positive letter-spacing on headings. Only Labels (+0.05em) and Micro (+0.02em) get positive tracking.

Motion

Do
  • Use the 5 defined easing curves. Every animation must use one of them.
  • Keep hover transitions at 200ms. Keep entrance animations at 800ms with 150ms stagger.
Don't
  • Use default CSS ease, ease-in-out, or linear for interactive animations.
  • Use bounce, spring, or elastic easing. Nothing overshoots. Reis IA motion is precise, not playful.

Brand Motifs

Do
  • Place the hourglass in sections about time, efficiency, urgency, and AI impact.
  • Use the hourglass as a 3-5% opacity watermark (200-300px) behind key sections.
Don't
  • Use any other icon as a brand watermark. The hourglass is the sole brand mark.
  • Animate the hourglass with rotation or flipping. It is a static, grounded symbol.
REIS IA

Design System v1.0 — the single source of truth for the Reis IA brand identity, tokens, components, and patterns.
